name: langgraph-checkpointer-pattern
tags: langgraph, checkpointer, SqliteSaver, thread_id, session_id, cross-hook, stateful-server,claude-hooks
body:
LangGraph SqliteSaver checkpointer wired into hooks/server.py. session_id maps to thread_id. UserPromptSubmit passes full _fresh_state() + event fields to establish checkpoint. PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop pass only event-specific fields — checkpointer restores the rest. prompt_id, domains, keywords, memories all retained in checkpoint across all four hook events. Zero DB reads between hooks.

Checkpoint DB: ~/.claude/langgraph_checkpoints.db (SqliteSaver, survives server restarts).

Why: LangServe was stateless — each invoke threw away SessionState. SqliteSaver makes the graph the session store and is cross-process safe. Learned from ACME checkpoint pattern (thread_id isolation, resume from checkpoint).
How to apply: Always pass only changed fields in non-UPS invocations. UserPromptSubmit is the only full state initializer (_fresh_state). When adding new hook logic that needs prior-turn data, read from state (it's in the checkpoint).
